inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed:
1. Leadership failure
2. Poor supervision
3. Poor sub-contractor control
4. PTW failure
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken: Unsafe work practices were suspended immediately. The CM of the cluster was called but he refused to visit the location.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
1. PTW must be controlled by the main contractor.
2. Close supervision of sub-contractors by main contractor shall be ensured.
3. Non-performing subcontractors shall not be paid and shall be removed from project.
4. Non-performing construction team shall be replaced without delays

inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed: No significant gap observed
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken: The team was commended for their visible leadership in driving transformation at Cluster 8A.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
The Construction Manager and his team were advised to ensure that sub-contractors do not mess up with their site. The following advise was given:
1. No payment for work if site condition is below the acceptable standard.
2. Close supervision of sub-contractors by the construction team of the main contractor.
3. Housekeeping shall be maintined for all shift.
4. Provide a temporary storage area for waste for each villa.
5. PTW shall remain under the control of the main contractor.

HSE Manager of TAJV

inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed:

PTW failure

inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken: High risk activity was suspended and workers removed from risk area. The PM and HSE team were invited and advised to ensure PTW implementation, close supervision and control of subcontractors.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
1.	Prepare Monthly lookahead programme for each package;
2.	Prepare Weekly lookahead programme for each cluster;
3.	Prepare Daily activity schedule for each cluster / block / villa
4.	Develop Supervision deployment plan identifying the responsible site engineers, supervisors, and HSE personnel for each work front.
5. Ensure Subcontractors' engagement in the project is strictly based on the above plans. No work shall commence without an approved PTW by the main contractor.
6. Communicate officially to all subcontractors that no activity shall commence without first reporting to the main contractor on daily basis.
7. Regularly share the above plans with KEO for close follow up and monitoring.

inspections.Workers_Observed: We observed a clear evidence of failed lift associated with lifting and installation of pre-cast manhole. No clear evidcne whether the lifting point of manholes were part of the design. This was a near miss case that was not reported and investigated.
inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed: Gap in design and planning
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken: The CM and team were advised to suspend all manhole related lifting operation until pull test is completed.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
1. Conduct a pull test on pre-cast man-hole before proceeding with lifting.
2. Demonstrate safe lifting plan for man-hole installation with the PSC team.

inspections.Workers_Observed: PTW was issued without the names of the authorized persons, no date or time was mentioned, no detailed description of task was included. This permit for work at the rooftop had already been renewed for 4 days without completing basic verification and review of site conditions. When investigated, it was observed that the verifier from the HSE team was an inspector that was not approved for the project. The inspector does not know the requirements of PTW.

inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed: PTW failure / inadequate field verification.
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken:
Excavation Safety: stop unsafe excavation activity, provide safe access/egress and rigid barricading/edge protection, and verify excavation controls at site.
PTW Field Verification: suspend the activity until a valid PTW is approved, physically verified, and communicated to the work crew.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
Excavation Safety: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure excavation inspection, access/egress, barricading, permit controls, and daily closeout verification are implemented.
PTW Field Verification: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall strengthen PTW ownership by the main contractor, including field verification before approval and monitoring during execution.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall assign competent supervision at the work front and verify subcontractor compliance continuously.

inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed:
1. Welfare Arrangement is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (0/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
2. Fire Readiness is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
3. Scaffolding Compliance is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken:
Welfare Arrangement: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
Fire Readiness: reinstate fire protection resources, access, and emergency readiness immediately.
Scaffolding Compliance: prevent scaffold use until competent inspection, correction, and valid tagging are completed.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
Welfare Arrangement: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Fire Readiness: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Scaffolding Compliance: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure scaffold planning, competent erection/inspection, tagging, and routine monitoring are enforced.

inspections.Likely_Root_Cause: Poor supervision / weak implementation of KPI controls
inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed:
1. Edge Protection scored 3/5 and requires management action.
2. Falling Object Prevention scored 3/5 and requires management action.
3. Excavation Safety scored 3/5 and requires management action.
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken:
Edge Protection: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
Falling Object Prevention: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
Excavation Safety: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
Edge Protection: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement corrective action, supervision and closeout evidence.

inspections.Top_3_Gaps_Observed:
1. Look ahead program not communicated.
2. PTW not implementated
3. Poor supervision
inspections.Immediate_Action_Taken: Excavation was suspended and the project team were advised on the safe approach to execute work.
inspections.Preventive_Action_Required:
Before excavation work begins, the contractor shall do the following:
1. Communicate lookahead program to the HSE team on time.
2. Ensure arrnagement for safe cross over platform and trench protection is on site before applying for excavation permit.
3. Review that arrnagement for cross over platform and trench protection is suitable and sufficient.
4. Use excavated earth to create bund walls simultaneously during excavation.
5. For excavations around villas, ensure all exit doors are blocked with hard barriers before starting excvation.
6. Do not approve excavation if the above arrangemnts are not reasonably in place.

1. Ensure operators understand and can read pre-use inspection checklist.
2. Closely supervise subcontractors and verify controls before approving PTW.

capa.gaps:

2. Poor supervision
3. Poor sub-contractor control
4. PTW failure
capa.immediate: Unsafe work practices were suspended immediately. The CM of the cluster was called but he refused to visit the location.
capa.preventive:
1. PTW must be controlled by the main contractor.
2. Close supervision of sub-contractors by main contractor shall be ensured.
3. Non-performing subcontractors shall not be paid and shall be removed from project.
4. Non-performing construction team shall be replaced without delays

1-3rd recommendation by the cluster CM
4th recommendation by the PD.

capa.gaps: No significant gap observed
capa.immediate: The team was commended for their visible leadership in driving transformation at Cluster 8A.
capa.preventive:
The Construction Manager and his team were advised to ensure that sub-contractors do not mess up with their site. The following advise was given:
1. No payment for work if site condition is below the acceptable standard.
2. Close supervision of sub-contractors by the construction team of the main contractor.
3. Housekeeping shall be maintined for all shift.
4. Provide a temporary storage area for waste for each villa.
5. PTW shall remain under the control of the main contractor.

capa.immediate: High risk activity was suspended and workers removed from risk area. The PM and HSE team were invited and advised to ensure PTW implementation, close supervision and control of subcontractors.
capa.preventive:
1.	Prepare Monthly lookahead programme for each package;
2.	Prepare Weekly lookahead programme for each cluster;
3.	Prepare Daily activity schedule for each cluster / block / villa
4.	Develop Supervision deployment plan identifying the responsible site engineers, supervisors, and HSE personnel for each work front.
5. Ensure Subcontractors' engagement in the project is strictly based on the above plans. No work shall commence without an approved PTW by the main contractor.
6. Communicate officially to all subcontractors that no activity shall commence without first reporting to the main contractor on daily basis.
7. Regularly share the above plans with KEO for close follow up and monitoring.

capa.gaps: PTW failure / inadequate field verification.
capa.immediate:
Excavation Safety: stop unsafe excavation activity, provide safe access/egress and rigid barricading/edge protection, and verify excavation controls at site.
PTW Field Verification: suspend the activity until a valid PTW is approved, physically verified, and communicated to the work crew.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
capa.preventive:
Excavation Safety: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure excavation inspection, access/egress, barricading, permit controls, and daily closeout verification are implemented.
PTW Field Verification: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall strengthen PTW ownership by the main contractor, including field verification before approval and monitoring during execution.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall assign competent supervision at the work front and verify subcontractor compliance continuously.

capa.gaps:
1. Welfare Arrangement is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (0/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
2. Fire Readiness is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
3. Scaffolding Compliance is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
capa.immediate:
Welfare Arrangement: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
Fire Readiness: reinstate fire protection resources, access, and emergency readiness immediately.
Scaffolding Compliance: prevent scaffold use until competent inspection, correction, and valid tagging are completed.
capa.preventive:
Welfare Arrangement: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Fire Readiness: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Scaffolding Compliance: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure scaffold planning, competent erection/inspection, tagging, and routine monitoring are enforced.

capa.rootCause: Poor supervision / weak implementation of KPI controls
capa.gaps:
1. Edge Protection scored 3/5 and requires management action.
2. Falling Object Prevention scored 3/5 and requires management action.
3. Excavation Safety scored 3/5 and requires management action.
capa.immediate:
Edge Protection: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
Falling Object Prevention: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
Excavation Safety: stop/hold affected activity where required and rectify immediate unsafe condition.
capa.preventive:
Edge Protection: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement corrective action, supervision and closeout evidence.
Falling Object Prevention: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement corrective action, supervision and closeout evidence.
Excavation Safety: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement corrective action, supervision and closeout evidence.

capa.gaps:
1. Look ahead program not communicated.
2. PTW not implementated
3. Poor supervision
capa.immediate: Excavation was suspended and the project team were advised on the safe approach to execute work.
capa.preventive:
Before excavation work begins, the contractor shall do the following:
1. Communicate lookahead program to the HSE team on time.
2. Ensure arrnagement for safe cross over platform and trench protection is on site before applying for excavation permit.
3. Review that arrnagement for cross over platform and trench protection is suitable and sufficient.
4. Use excavated earth to create bund walls simultaneously during excavation.
5. For excavations around villas, ensure all exit doors are blocked with hard barriers before starting excvation.
6. Do not approve excavation if the above arrangemnts are not reasonably in place.

capa.gaps:
1. Lack of competence
2. PTW not properly implementated
3. Poor supervision
capa.immediate: MEWP activity was suspended
capa.preventive:

2. Closely supervise subcontractors and verify controls before approving PTW.
